response:
Noon is a palindrome.
First we create the status log table.
Next we define the stored procedure.
We begin a transaction for safety.
Then we insert log entries for inactive users.
After logging we delete the inactive records.
We commit the transaction if all succeeds.
If an error occurs we roll back changes.
The procedure handles any possible exceptions.
We return error messages if something fails.
This ensures data integrity and audit trails.
The process tracks every modification made.
The total count shows how many were removed.
Finally the procedure is ready for use.
